evals.name: full-constitution-with-role
evals.prompt: Create an agent constitution for data-validator that transforms raw CSV datasets into validated, typed schemas
evals.expected_output: Complete agent.md with all 22 fields filled, specific to data validation domain, with measurable mission, typed inputs/outputs, and 3+ failure modes.
expectations: Output contains all 22 fields (identity 3, authority 6, governance 6, quality 7)
Mission includes measurable outcome specific to data validation
Non-Goals has 3+ items with responsible alternative agents named
Allowed Tools lists specific tool names, no wildcards
Failure Handling table has 3+ modes with detection, response, fallback columns

evals.name: interview-mode-no-role
evals.prompt: Create an agent constitution for onboarding-agent
evals.expected_output: Enters interview mode asking 3 questions: primary responsibility, ecosystem context, tool access. Does not generate constitution until answers provided.
expectations: Asks about primary responsibility before generating
Asks about existing agents in the ecosystem
Does not generate a complete constitution from the name alone

evals.name: ecosystem-awareness
evals.prompt: Create a constitution for report-generator agent. We already have data-analyst, chart-builder, and email-sender agents.
evals.expected_output: Constitution references existing agents in delegation rules, escalation, and non-goals. Boundaries are explicitly drawn against data-analyst and chart-builder.
expectations: Non-Goals reference data-analyst for data transformation tasks
Delegation Rules specify when to delegate to chart-builder for visualization
Escalation Rules include path to human for business decisions
Scope explicitly excludes what data-analyst and chart-builder handle

evals.name: false-positive-not-agent
evals.prompt: Write a Python function that validates user input
evals.expected_output: Does not generate an agent constitution. This is a code request, not an agent design request.
expectations: Does not trigger agent constitution generation
Recognizes this as a coding task, not an agent design task

evals.name: overlapping-scope-detection
evals.prompt: Create a constitution for data-processor. We already have data-analyst that handles data transformation.
evals.expected_output: Flags the scope overlap between data-processor and data-analyst. Asks for clarification on boundaries before generating.
expectations: Detects overlap between data-processor and existing data-analyst
Asks user to clarify boundaries before proceeding
Does not generate a constitution with overlapping scope without resolution
